name: Dr. Annie Andrews
subject: They’re teenagers just like any other, but politicians continue to target them→
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THEIR FUTURE PAC
body:
| | | If you'd like to unsubscribe,[click here.](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/P8Elou2Rvc0qoMPEUZrMXcOpy_XqN4R2ZGjoK-5zZnl7NBjmTZL5F5oqdT0MFW4GLNp5FjRArZ2gECnWf63UZWzcCuYR-Es9PjYmAXR4JxZbuH_t8O228MjZNOxN7Lm2WoL5eKG3lZx13kI8WltFNEeVbmRzcgA53a7Y1SoI4qXxs5suGE136UZD-cE2cVkwbTNOImIzBGFMWAEAx2etbx1SZ-pD1YAXxROnMo069yRAXNcKWQpIGkzj9pTdA95ME0zaz7KwKNbwh-OAZcqjeM76Lg-7DrsevkqNpBk2AYqn7J3vWm8LOKzya7RMCL1yPOZnmbyJee9Gbe4WpiFYiWcd1cPIpD_kFtvSTv-5yVQ0Grvhj755t9J8Zcd0sDUKZZDu2OXq5_PT2o3-OS_6RuyVawSDDPyJS6-KSK-iwkoyGz0_AyIppdjA9hfADVKzYZZELD4J0RXpkp9t-xF4XgZcDFk9HBPWMKEDVghe-e4fofLSUy-3KK3dIi2lwQrXM7VQGhBQOVeNII4GigrH7k01xJQIfyiALPDHN2YP_eWzWa8mh7nsOWrJwOXFvZ4OHstMvHZI4NyN5HCUE35ktc3eLZCP6-rx8TzW_PHzOp8/3wv/eIO5f490TYONeoAdEcdECA/h0/zR5OkJrDKdarEoEswvAw8hiNgPzYk-Psk9tyqd5uzY4) | | June is Pride Month and I want to take this time to talk to you about why we all should care about the attacks taking place against trans kids.

Practicing pediatric medicine, this issue is critical to me. I’ll never forget walking into a patient room a few years ago to meet a teenage boy who had been admitted for attempting to kill himself. He was transgender and his parents didn’t support his gender identity. While he waited for a bed to be available at an inpatient psychiatric hospital, he suffered more intrusive thoughts, believing everyone would be happier if he was gone.

This isn’t an uncommon story. This is why I’m asking you to join me in writing a letter to our state reps to demand they stop targeting trans kids with hateful legislation. **_[Click here to start writing.](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/P8Elou2Rvc0qoMPEUZrMXcOpy_XqN4R2ZGjoK-5zZnk2aPz0xx7qbPDkb_Dorpgpvl-pW8pr-kr_dPDju_oj4j5AYOy7N7ATlfsd8Bo8L5SCAhbHfl2bgTErBM4Kugy42YVz6i872WE7E3-A5TKPNE5DjIArTTY0V-tR5rdNwuMeRDKQiPI5jd1lgFEfuIrVukMqd2Ia8hXurCqHXtAoPp9_hIs10dM6GparGW79w0aAzMNEtxjTvKZ_-nk_P8F-2cI4RVX477XlIhCdOBNnkP_Nx5w17n_ibVqzXYfrGuw3f32nc1CQgTh4UKnAfEgXcgyi_k2PaCxaIoytDWUBo4xhvOGjF8YQasH7j2SxjgKYbaYcwW8p5iF-BijpwgpD_erCmLSOkC8arlp-kNu3t1dhYOZ85Hhc3l0NcttbyU9OHYiHnrl7QrW4PuUYoFHxkcRkkvByAxUY0PC7oJRfkG0T5neju0TIFkF6lQMofSA/3wv/eIO5f490TYONeoAdEcdECA/h1/jiR29_fvjKY8Yta_tnPInHJg6-JHwh2UPMnb39jb4QA)_**

Here’s the truth: Teenagers are struggling. Transgender teenagers are struggling even more. They want love and acceptance just like every other teenager you know.

Some of them get it at home, some of them do not. Some of them get it at school, some of them do not. Some of them have a supportive adult in their lives, some of them do not.
Here’s what I know: The GOP does not care about transgender people, transgender kids, or gender-affirming care. They care about winning elections. They understand the political power of “othering” the most vulnerable in society, and, right now, that is trans kids.

State laws like South Carolina’s transgender sports ban contribute to the ongoing stigmatization of young people who are just trying to navigate their teenage years. These kids deserve love and acceptance, not bullying.

This isn’t about transgender kids. This is about science. This is about compassion. This is about the abuse of political power.

**_[Will you stand up for trans kids this Pride Month by signing a letter demanding accountability from state reps who have supported anti-trans legislation?](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/P8Elou2Rvc0qoMPEUZrMXcOpy_XqN4R2ZGjoK-5zZnk2aPz0xx7qbPDkb_Dorpgpvl-pW8pr-kr_dPDju_oj4j5AYOy7N7ATlfsd8Bo8L5SCAhbHfl2bgTErBM4Kugy42YVz6i872WE7E3-A5TKPNGrVnBeo14ZxmJqeqdpRopZeqS6RRYRhtG683b4Z8hKirfeLrs5lJ_HPOVVHRZ-FWMQ3AqqmQRQybt5x4E1ptnfSCCO4XgiYtKSJJEVZIz3Bql4YrhoVUz70jc-WIJEywFY0R0XipMlisMlyJEHvJekCUDl_VtAO29cCMMZpqhErfYBe4jccb7mJR5AJb_QSeWEpDZKdLOZq51Wfs42VW8fQhMO8dJ5IfUGEANZMsTy_r1_NiQHQg2InnLQfXdUOAV3cxgHCIib9h8cDqyWTProh83fM2MAhWBIIsTgUHlxFzvwaC69xmu0GHaMHn3qy2IDxoJXpw7ZBmwDlSTAgHaM/3wv/eIO5f490TYONeoAdEcdECA/h2/bPVGeKvj_5IqIM6iiMXy9NlD89bJweYcE8jMOMIj6Kg)_**

---

On the day my patient was getting ready to transfer to the psychiatric hospital, as I was leaving his room, I noticed him looking at my ID badge hanging around my neck. He saw my small pink, blue, and white transgender flag button. He looked at me, smiled, and said “I like your button.” It’s a small thing, but it mattered to him.

All kids deserve to have people in their corner who support them, not have their existence be belittled.

Thank you,

Annie

clean_body: | | | If you'd like to unsubscribe, | | June is Pride Month and I want to take this time to talk to you about why we all should care about the attacks taking place against trans kids. Practicing pediatric medicine, this issue is critical to me. I’ll never forget walking into a patient room a few years ago to meet a teenage boy who had been admitted for attempting to kill himself. He was transgender and his parents didn’t support his gender identity. While he waited for a bed to be available at an inpatient psychiatric hospital, he suffered more intrusive thoughts, believing everyone would be happier if he was gone. This isn’t an uncommon story. This is why I’m asking you to join me in writing a letter to our state reps to demand they stop targeting trans kids with hateful legislation. Here’s the truth: Teenagers are struggling. Transgender teenagers are struggling even more. They want love and acceptance just like every other teenager you know. Some of them get it at home, some of them do not. Some of them get it at school, some of them do not. Some of them have a supportive adult in their lives, some of them do not. Here’s what I know: The GOP does not care about transgender people, transgender kids, or gender-affirming care. They care about winning elections. They understand the political power of “othering” the most vulnerable in society, and, right now, that is trans kids. State laws like South Carolina’s transgender sports ban contribute to the ongoing stigmatization of young people who are just trying to navigate their teenage years. These kids deserve love and acceptance, not bullying. This isn’t about transgender kids. This is about science. This is about compassion. This is about the abuse of political power. ** | | On the day my patient was getting ready to transfer to the psychiatric hospital, as I was leaving his room, I noticed him looking at my ID badge hanging around my neck. He saw my small pink, blue, and white transgender flag button. He looked at me, smiled, and said “I like your button.” It’s a small thing, but it mattered to him. All kids deserve to have people in their corner who support them, not have their existence be belittled. | | | [Yes](https://go.dscc.org/go/14161?ask=5&t=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C25%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20230607-BidenAlert-BidenSocialSecurityApprovalFTN-ETG-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20230607-BidenAlert-BidenSocialSecurityApprovalFTN-ETG-sl1&akid=11005%2E9716520%2E79uZOZ)

Team, President Biden just scored a HUGE win for Social Security:

**FIRST:** The GOP took back the House and immediately tried to push ways to GUT Social Security.

**THEN:** President Biden flatly refused, declaring ANY Social Security cuts off the table.

**AND NOW:** Speaker McCarthy says Biden "walled off" programs like Social Security from the GOP's cuts -- _**stopping them in their tracks!**_

But this fight isn't over: McCarthy ALREADY says he'll look at cutting Social Security _again_ , so we have to speak up NOW.

**Our urgent Live Poll will show that Democrats everywhere are standing with President Biden as he fights to protect Social Security -- but only if we reach our 50,000 response goal!**

**Please, tell us now:****Do you approve of President Biden's actions to defend Social Security?**

body:
| | | [](http://links.unleashamericapac.co/u/click?_t=4fd2065bb87d4411bcd7523509144268&_m=a939a0a6c62949a7b7b738372ad3a41f&_e=x8c-Wnrh8haC-N-iKbourXVcF5MrReVXi9OWQhYLWS46Z-UD2VS7w-ZbYSYkrcdEoMF77UNmMEcYXBNN4mo6ucYRAVxcJBHV2533Z7l3PexIgVEfv7sQuCEGtDPQXrugMZEVsT9LQZk_jbn9IdcI7SEKLgFgjzgx35AMkO7jJ9oBzBfOCDuIl62TcetNKOAE-stSRN4SE6D1Dvyq_NOlKA_gjjYM9Oj5-PUVOm8T2kZ7fkvMjfCvlAblvhPPOWq3-qARzWyyUYygiplTyjcuxDsabh1tmFmcHyWGKjN9ASM%3D)
---
| | June 7, 2023
---
| WEEKLY NEWSLETTER
---
| Issue 13
---
| | **A Note from CAPT Hung Cao USN (RET)** Over the last week, lawmakers in Washington were working through the night to reach an agreement on spending cuts and voted on a new debt ceiling agreement to suspend the debt ceiling until January 1, 2025.

We have to take responsibility for our actions and we need to elect leaders in Virginia and across the country who will rein in Washington’s reckless spending. If we max out our credit card, we don't get a new credit card with a higher limit and transfer the balance to the new credit card. This is essentially what raising the debt ceiling means.

While working to balance the Navy’s $140 billion budget, for every “add” we had to find an “offset.” Simply put, if we wanted a new missile, we would have to buy less bombs or rockets, but at the end of the day, we balanced the budget based on our guidelines. It is time to live within our means and tighten the belt. \- CAPT Hung Cao USN (RET), Honorary Chairman of [Unleash America PAC](http://links.unleashamericapac.co/u/click?_t=4fd2065bb87d4411bcd7523509144268&_m=a939a0a6c62949a7b7b738372ad3a41f&_e=x8c-Wnrh8haC-N-iKbourXVcF5MrReVXi9OWQhYLWS46Z-UD2VS7w-ZbYSYkrcdEoMF77UNmMEcYXBNN4mo6ucYRAVxcJBHV2533Z7l3PexIgVEfv7sQuCEGtDPQXrugMZEVsT9LQZk_jbn9IdcI7ZcGVeYsDqGga69uicL4pAY0VWXMRNTJXTniAfOHTOrOaSQHbM4vCqHQNymCRyyPQK-Mywc8dftqi_LVAlW8mo1uuXDY3_8BTV2P569QnBf3EHMy3DpdMa_iyCiKlhRNBvV66MFfO3lOQNzJoAy-cMs%3D)

clean_body: | | | | | June 7, 2023 | WEEKLY NEWSLETTER | Issue 13 | | A Note from CAPT Hung Cao USN (RET) Over the last week, lawmakers in Washington were working through the night to reach an agreement on spending cuts and voted on a new debt ceiling agreement to suspend the debt ceiling until January 1, 2025. We have to take responsibility for our actions and we need to elect leaders in Virginia and across the country who will rein in Washington’s reckless spending. If we max out our credit card, we don't get a new credit card with a higher limit and transfer the balance to the new credit card. This is essentially what raising the debt ceiling means. While working to balance the Navy’s $140 billion budget, for every “add” we had to find an “offset.” Simply put, if we wanted a new missile, we would have to buy less bombs or rockets, but at the end of the day, we balanced the budget based on our guidelines. It is time to live within our means and tighten the belt. \- CAPT Hung Cao USN (RET), Honorary Chairman of | | | Social Media to Share Here are a few of our top posts from social media this week. Be sure to like and share, and when you tweet at Hung remember to use #UnleashAmerica! | | | | | | That's it for this week, thank you for reading. Be sure to follow Unleash America on social media! | | | ---|---|--- | | Paid for by Unleash America PAC. name: Zak Malamed
subject: What’s going on with the air outside
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ZAK FOR CONGRESS
body:
Friends are wearing masks outside. It’s tough to breathe. When you step out the door, there’s a noticeable gray haze.
#
It seems like everyone — at least on the East Coast — is talking about the air today. Friends are wearing masks outside. It’s tough to breathe. When you step out the door, there’s a noticeable gray, sometimes even orange, haze.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/zm-email-20230607?refcode=em20230607&refcode2=97_8507_QuLzHr&akid=97%2E8507%2EQuLzHr)
I read that NYC has the worst air quality of any big city in the world today. And it all traces back to these massive wildfires in Canada.
You probably know that last year the Democrats passed the most consequential climate bill of all time. That’ll help us prevent big wildfires in the future. **But we have to do more in Congress to hit the goals that scientists have laid out for us to avert the worst of these disasters.**
And the sad truth is that with climate deniers like George Santos and the Republicans holding the House majority, there’s absolutely no hope of passing anything serious. But another big push on climate change is within our grasp _if_ we take back the House in 2024 and elect a new generation of Democratic leaders.
I bring all this up to see if you can help me flip NY’s 3rd District from red to blue. Let’s get George Santos out of there. Let’s get me in there. And then let’s take more serious action to curb climate change so our kids and grandkids can live a healthy future with clean air. **Are you in?**
[**Will you add a $5 contribution to help us defeat the MAGA Madness and flip our district? Every dollar we raise brings us closer to a win that will get me on the House floor so I can vote ‘YES’ on bold climate action.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/zm-email-20230607?refcode=em20230607&refcode2=97_8507_QuLzHr&akid=97%2E8507%2EQuLzHr)
**[Donate $5 ››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/zm-email-20230607?amount=5&refcode=em20230607&refcode2=97_8507_QuLzHr&akid=97%2E8507%2EQuLzHr)**
When you see the haze in the air today or hear about the fires, it’s tempting to give up on a political process that has failed us for so long. Some people feel hopeless, and I get it.
But I haven’t lost faith in what we can do with government if we come together. And the climate bill that Democrats passed just last year should give us all a little affirmation. We can do hard things if we put in the work.
[**Please make a donation today to help us keep up that fight.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/zm-email-20230607?amount=5&refcode=em20230607&refcode2=97_8507_QuLzHr&akid=97%2E8507%2EQuLzHr)
With gratitude,
Zak Malamed

name: Rashida Tlaib
subject: “I was very excited to not only learn more aboutwhat’s going on in my community, but also to learn aboutmyself”
body:
In the first 5 months of the 2023 youth fellowship, participants have already completed 340 hours of field work in their communities. | | |
---
Friend, Even as right-wing hate is attempting to tear our country apart, **I am still optimistic about our future**. This is because young people are more engaged in politics today than any time since the Civil Rights Movement of the 1950s and ‘60s. And, this younger generation is the most diverse of any in U.S. history. **Through our Rooted in Community Leadership PAC, we’ve developed a youth fellowship program to train young people of diverse backgrounds, ages 14 – 24 on the skills to be effective organizers on a range of issues impacting our communities.** This year’s fellows are 100% BIPOC with 85% identifying as women, 7% men, and 7% non-binary. Past graduates have gone on to be student government leaders on their college campuses, worked as local and regional organizers on youth engagement and student GOTV efforts, and worked in areas ranging from environmental justice to Palestinian liberation. **Here’s what a few Youth Fellowship graduates said about their experiences:**
> _“I was very excited to not only learn more about what’s going on in my community, but also to learn about myself more―being able to speak in front of people and learn how to engage with my peers.”_
> _“[The fellowship] thoughtfully and safely [created] an environment for all of us to get together and talk about our field work, social issues, and our lives. It's really a great thing that the Tlaib Team has put together.”_
> _“Being in this fellowship, I have now gone out of my way to connect with community activities and others doing great things for our community.”_
In the first 5 months of the 2023 youth fellowship, participants have already completed 340 hours of field work in their communities while also receiving instruction from local community organizers about criminal legal reform, the history of the labor movement and union organizing, environmental justice and access to clean water, education justice, and gun violence prevention. Organizing young people did not happen overnight. It’s taken a multi-year, year-round organizing strategy to train and mobilize young people.
Younger voters are concerned about the climate crisis, workers’ rights, college affordability, access to clean water, housing, LGBTQ+ rights, immigrant rights, abortion rights, and racial and social justice.**In order to advance our shared agenda, we must continue to invest in youth leadership.**

clean_body: In the first 5 months of the 2023 youth fellowship, participants have already completed 340 hours of field work in their communities. | | | Friend, Even as right-wing hate is attempting to tear our country apart, I am still optimistic about our future. This is because young people are more engaged in politics today than any time since the Civil Rights Movement of the 1950s and ‘60s. And, this younger generation is the most diverse of any in U.S. history. Through our Rooted in Community Leadership PAC, we’ve developed a youth fellowship program to train young people of diverse backgrounds, ages 14 – 24 on the skills to be effective organizers on a range of issues impacting our communities. This year’s fellows are 100% BIPOC with 85% identifying as women, 7% men, and 7% non-binary. Past graduates have gone on to be student government leaders on their college campuses, worked as local and regional organizers on youth engagement and student GOTV efforts, and worked in areas ranging from environmental justice to Palestinian liberation. Here’s what a few Youth Fellowship graduates said about their experiences: “I was very excited to not only learn more about what’s going on in my community, but also to learn about myself more―being able to speak in front of people and learn how to engage with my peers.” “[The fellowship] thoughtfully and safely [created] an environment for all of us to get together and talk about our field work, social issues, and our lives. It's really a great thing that the Tlaib Team has put together.” “Being in this fellowship, I have now gone out of my way to connect with community activities and others doing great things for our community.” In the first 5 months of the 2023 youth fellowship, participants have already completed 340 hours of field work in their communities while also receiving instruction from local community organizers about criminal legal reform, the history of the labor movement and union organizing, environmental justice and access to clean water, education justice, and gun violence prevention. | Organizing young people did not happen overnight. It’s taken a multi-year, year-round organizing strategy to train and mobilize young people. Younger voters are concerned about the climate crisis, workers’ rights, college affordability, access to clean water, housing, LGBTQ+ rights, immigrant rights, abortion rights, and racial and social justice.In order to advance our shared agenda, we must continue to invest in youth leadership. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately: | | | | | | | | | Young people are ready to lead now! Together, we’re providing them with the tools to be successful for decades to come. Thank you, Rashida | Rooted in Community Leadership PAC, PO Box 15845, Washington, DC 20003, not authorized by any candidate or candidates committee. | | Sent via . name: Katie Porter HQ
subject: A look inside the CA Dem Convention
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Senate
body:
A few weeks ago we emailed and asked for your support in helping Team Porter pull off a successful California Democratic Party Convention.
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/14558?t=1&akid=24367%2E3422442%2E9z9NR7)
Derek,
A few weeks ago we emailed and asked for your support in helping Team Porter pull off a successful California Democratic Party Convention. **Thanks to 123 supporters who stepped up and made a donation in response to that email,** we were able to outfit volunteers and make a strong showing.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230607-2?refcode=EMAI07_2_CON_20230607_V1_F&refcode2=24367_3422442_9z9NR7&akid=24367%2E3422442%2E9z9NR7)
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230607-2?refcode=EMAI07_2_CON_20230607_V1_F&refcode2=24367_3422442_9z9NR7&akid=24367%2E3422442%2E9z9NR7)
We’re only able to run our campaign because of grassroots supporters. **[Are you able to help us be prepared for future events and a competitive and costly race ahead?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230607-2?refcode=EMAI07_2_CON_20230607_V1_F&refcode2=24367_3422442_9z9NR7&akid=24367%2E3422442%2E9z9NR7)**
**[Yes, I’ll donate.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230607-2?refcode=EMAI07_2_CON_20230607_V1_F&refcode2=24367_3422442_9z9NR7&akid=24367%2E3422442%2E9z9NR7)**
Thanks for being a part of Team Porter.
* * *
From: info@katieporter.com
Subject line: A big moment for our campaign
Sent: Wednesday, May 17
Derek, over Memorial Day weekend, Katie and our team will be in Los Angeles for the California Democratic Party Convention. The convention is an opportunity for Katie to meet with potential supporters, party delegates, and state and local officials.
**The convention is a big moment for our campaign.** Press and political pundits will be watching to see which candidate seems to be garnering energy and excitement, and that can translate into very real momentum for our campaign _outside_ of the convention.
But pulling off a successful convention weekend doesn't come cheap. We have to purchase thousands of buttons, stickers, and t-shirts, and print literature about Katie. **Here are just a few of the expenses:**
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230607-2?refcode=EMAI07_2_CON_20230607_V1_F&refcode2=24367_3422442_9z9NR7&akid=24367%2E3422442%2E9z9NR7)
Want to support Team Porter at this month's Democratic Party Convention? **[Consider chipping in today to help power our campaign.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230607-2?refcode=EMAI07_2_CON_20230607_V1_F&refcode2=24367_3422442_9z9NR7&akid=24367%2E3422442%2E9z9NR7)**
**[Donate $5 ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230607-2?amount=5&refcode=EMAI07_2_CON_20230607_V1_F&refcode2=24367_3422442_9z9NR7&akid=24367%2E3422442%2E9z9NR7)**
Thanks for considering!
Team KP

Katie is running for the U.S. Senate because Californians deserve a warrior fighting for them in Washington. Our campaign is people-powered, we don’t take a dime from corporate PACs, federal lobbyists, or executives from Big Oil or Big Pharma. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-footer?refcode=EMAI07_2_CON_20230607_V1_F&t=2&refcode2=24367_3422442_9z9NR7&akid=24367%2E3422442%2E9z9NR7)**
---
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/receive-fewer-kp4s-emails/?t=3&akid=24367%2E3422442%2E9z9NR7)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/kp4s-em-update-info-footer)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.katieporter.com/unsubscribe/kp4s-unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=24367%2E3422442%2E9z9NR7)

To give by check:
Katie Porter for Senate
PO Box 5176
Irvine, CA 92616
Email us: [info@katieporter.com](mailto:info@katieporter.com)

disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fight for Progress PAC and not authorized by any candidate or any candidate's committee

I wanted to take a moment to tell you a bit about why I’m in this fight.

(Me getting a fresh cut)

Hey folks, happy Wednesday!

I wanted to take a moment to tell you a bit about why I’m in this fight.

I’m not the kind of guy you’d expect to see in Congress. I still get $12 flattop haircuts. And at home on the farm, I butcher my own meat.

I didn’t come to Washington to play into partisan hogwash—I came because I didn’t see anyone in our government sticking up for rural communities like mine.

I’m running to continue to deliver for Montanans, from holding our government accountable to standing up for veterans and protecting the Montana way of life.

**But with Mitch McConnell and his dark money backers hellbent on taking back the Senate majority and targeting my seat to make their pipe dream a reality, I’ll need all the help I can get from my grassroots team.**

[_**Will you split a donation of any amount today between my campaign and Fight for Progress to help protect our Democratic majority and keep me fighting in the Senate for hardworking Montanans and their families?**_](https://click.everyaction.com/k/64497856/410272500/665036970?refcode=BLU-FFP-20230607-1-EM&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNS8xLzc3NDA2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjY3ODVmOTU2LTk2MDUtZWUxMS05MDdjLTAwMjI0ODMyZWI3MyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=_F1IDY4bt0Byoljnen8sOxSf201uFaA-iEUQqwxlEns=)

clean_body: I wanted to take a moment to tell you a bit about why I’m in this fight. | = (Me getting a fresh cut) Hey folks, happy Wednesday! I wanted to take a moment to tell you a bit about why I’m in this fight. I’m not the kind of guy you’d expect to see in Congress. I still get $12 flattop haircuts. And at home on the farm, I butcher my own meat. I didn’t come to Washington to play into partisan hogwash—I came because I didn’t see anyone in our government sticking up for rural communities like mine. I’m running to continue to deliver for Montanans, from holding our government accountable to standing up for veterans and protecting the Montana way of life. But with Mitch McConnell and his dark money backers hellbent on taking back the Senate majority and targeting my seat to make their pipe dream a reality, I’ll need all the help I can get from my grassroots team. = | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately. | | = | = | = | = | = Thanks for chipping in. I’m grateful you’re in this fight with me. —Jon * | | = Paid for by Fight for Progress PAC and not authorized by any candidate or any candidate's committee. I wanted you to be one of the first to know: I just announced my candidacy for the U.S. Senate to defeat Josh Hawley.

[If you’re with me in the fight to bring real leadership back to Missouri and send Hawley packing, split a donation between The Collective PAC and my campaign today. Keep reading to learn why I'm running.](https://an.collectivepac.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953xKsysMWCjOH7z9zOi5HV3cSVSlVcFh_bZgnQPluJzKMGO1MdvKBMFPtCSijiwAFqrRyYkz4DtRKuqZpSMpYuRgg33F95l0izyJCSY02LoJFXHPSUXsCCP9H7aZ7Gtenx8WG5r4osieyihCSm2mRC3cdJGNJm_G2h0zLJPZFqlAtnTo0UptSQJfE9tQJGhY8uyb90J5IZ83vRqdANXLvq5nq009HojYVD7xktVO4Ig9p7xZeSwd5ihPVtp6TMotQhqoDLi1vvQwZBzV3o7RymUYkikIZ2Bp4WgScLizNEScGNwFfkG5Bqsg_fAZ8zSd7ELWpc3rjDd7wJ0m_fzVLSaKkE58ey_DC8LxoykBM_E8XcWE4tKNzNNK8xnSzhxI54iIrkFWFTqzf50LlsG_HT1B1jGfYa6bf6F2p3ac0Apgerv0GEvvGYtoI-MVwbvzV7TxvjdfFfnrRHCopYLbfbLIlyWPiW_hgZWjQLcQ2DTvwjO9-3vLeZdq5BUVy5TcUu9jxJosgHDG0PQc73UNaXcwmJQJ8fvaVH7Nl1lkcai1iA/3wv/tq8z5CYgRiuY8iDA8RV7wg/h2/5OrhJiaIxnexsLQX7d3F0qQ6uPhhx5b-rgoxeIzvjuE)

For me — this fight is personal. My dad spent 25 years as a police officer, and growing up, my family taught me I had a responsibility to make a difference when I could. In Ferguson, Missouri, that meant trying to help calm tensions between police and protesters.

I did what was natural to me: I worked to mediate between two sides who seemed at war with each other. At the time, I was a part-time judge and a professor, enforcing the law and teaching it. But Ferguson taught me I could be doing more — so I ran for Ferguson City Council and _won._

Then, when I saw a lack of leadership in the St. Louis County prosecutor's office, I took on the Democratic political establishment. In 2018, we shocked the nation when I was elected the first African American top prosecutor in the largest prosecuting attorney’s office in Missouri. With your help and support, we defeated a seven-term incumbent while taking a tough stand against violent criminals, all while bringing power closer to our community.

Now, I'm seeing that same lack of leadership I saw in St. Louis County in Washington. Public service is in my DNA, and I'm ready to fix it. That's why I need you onboard in our fight to defeat Josh Hawley in 2024:

[If you’re ready to send Hawley packing, split a donation between The Collective PAC and my campaign today to help us get this campaign off the ground!](https://an.collectivepac.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953xKsysMWCjOH7z9zOi5HV3cSVSlVcFh_bZgnQPluJzKMGO1MdvKBMFPtCSijiwAFqrRyYkz4DtRKuqZpSMpYuRgg33F95l0izyJCSY02LoJFXHPSUXsCCP9H7aZ7Gtenx8WG5r4osieyihCSm2mRC3cdJGNJm_G2h0zLJPZFqlAtnTo0UptSQJfE9tQJGhY8uyb90J5IZ83vRqdANXLvq5nq009HojYVD7xktVO4Ig9p7xZeSwd5ihPVtp6TMotQhqoDLi1vvQwZBzV3o7RymUYkV8H8thPI_24wohce1eicSIsNLOZ-gJ8HHGLHU-N46A6CI-_wdRMosMmxQgNPA0KnTWLiG1of_ZvR-Jae_IAW4jRYDp4FhKTAB64tuljLojfuHfin5TR5A8lVzfIcGojbIzsT6Ld9lSgKkQrzdPny870X51QiCAGEj3HYxiiDIVLaLA_E8IKZr0Q35h5LPJOkZZ-1By_Sqq0mfpiAZBdEYZTGYkwzMfNW5NXtURUD2h2hNS-wQQBrV76RBAcO_JiSVPCt4ASzoVADqoZ7Vi1cQ/3wv/tq8z5CYgRiuY8iDA8RV7wg/h3/QHQlGjQeGhRmsLmAx4L0oD5vjOp2qjRBoD1VKaGvCkA) | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately and split between Wesley Bell and The Collective PAC.

Peter, since Day One in office, _Hawley’s only been focused on himself._

**We can do better.**

I know we’re going to face many challenges on the road ahead. There will be a lot of doubters who won’t believe we can win. But here’s what I believe: We have more in common than politicians like Hawley say we do, and together, we can take our state back.

[If you’re tired of politicians ignoring people like you, then this campaign is YOUR campaign. Let’s send Josh Hawley packing. I hope I can count on your support to help get our campaign off the ground. Split a donation today!](https://an.collectivepac.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953xKsysMWCjOH7z9zOi5HV3cSVSlVcFh_bZgnQPluJzKMGO1MdvKBMFPtCSijiwAFqrRyYkz4DtRKuqZpSMpYuRgg33F95l0izyJCSY02LoJFXHPSUXsCCP9H7aZ7Gtenx8WG5r4osieyihCSm2mRC3cdJGNJm_G2h0zLJPZFqlAtnTo0UptSQJfE9tQJGhY8uyb90J5IZ83vRqdANXLvq5nq009HojYVD7xktVO4Ig9p7xZeSwd5ihPVtp6TMotQhqoDLi1vvQwZBzV3o7RymUYuuPZUuXLzKG96RF9kBhvVfa10AkRug5_aFV1s2jHtHuGhTu1yrKLu4rbCkT29i-qE7lvMbkAu9Bc-xOWWqFTIPVwmPJ7Ns3qBIaaqXMjg5NocPDN60lqesc60Ap_1TomvG-zflfD6YfzGw7keF9L2qMdx5Eb9AflOyVeGmzK2jZ8O0o3YgW0qWK8vi4dbMHXrlZ37A_alBR1VBnFvSFI0VJAlvzOOMKSHObYTsBSV8Q9qCfJhhQ7HQWyd6Yg3vHQKvwBQUC2SLXSkunTlBnjwA/3wv/tq8z5CYgRiuY8iDA8RV7wg/h10/vthvQiSfGj0LUSfAsf8sMTWQwp__PL66EFHfrSkhpgM)

clean_body: | | If you want to help The Collective PAC build Black political power up and down the ballot, and across the country, then you’re in the right place. If you'd like to unsubscribe | | Hey Peter, I wanted you to be one of the first to know: I just announced my candidacy for the U.S. Senate to defeat Josh Hawley. For me — this fight is personal. My dad spent 25 years as a police officer, and growing up, my family taught me I had a responsibility to make a difference when I could. In Ferguson, Missouri, that meant trying to help calm tensions between police and protesters. I did what was natural to me: I worked to mediate between two sides who seemed at war with each other. At the time, I was a part-time judge and a professor, enforcing the law and teaching it. But Ferguson taught me I could be doing more — so I ran for Ferguson City Council and won. Then, when I saw a lack of leadership in the St. Louis County prosecutor's office, I took on the Democratic political establishment. In 2018, we shocked the nation when I was elected the first African American top prosecutor in the largest prosecuting attorney’s office in Missouri. With your help and support, we defeated a seven-term incumbent while taking a tough stand against violent criminals, all while bringing power closer to our community. Now, I'm seeing that same lack of leadership I saw in St. Louis County in Washington. Public service is in my DNA, and I'm ready to fix it. That's why I need you onboard in our fight to defeat Josh Hawley in 2024: | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately and split between Wesley Bell and The Collective PAC. | | | | | | Peter, since Day One in office, Hawley’s only been focused on himself. We can do better. I know we’re going to face many challenges on the road ahead. There will be a lot of doubters who won’t believe we can win. But here’s what I believe: We have more in common than politicians like Hawley say we do, and together, we can take our state back. Together we'll make a difference, Wesley The Collective PAC is strategically focused on creating a truly equitable democracy where our nation’s local, state and federal governments have diverse and talented elected leadership and Black people are fully represented in positions of power to create the policies necessary to progress our communities forward. Since our inception in 2016, we have helped 110 candidates win general elections at the local, state and federal levels. We strive to support the next wave of progressive and unapologetic Black candidates who will usher in a sea of change in our political system while helping America fulfill its promise of democracy by creating a government that will truly be for the people and of the people. To Donate by Check: PO Box 15320, DC 20003 Paid for by the Collective PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. ** | | Sent via . Did you see my team’s message? | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/64498156/410286054/1504078835?refcode=EM_FR_230607NetPensFwd_N_HF_Actives_3_G_N_Logo&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDA1NzQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiN2MzZjQ1NDgtOWMwNS1lZTExLTkwN2MtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=6tqAJBBtvZt4z13GNRBztp2-4DXo8jUdXQPCCK31s34=) | Peter,

Last week, I watched as some of the last net pens were pulled from Puget Sound – a vital step to supporting our environment and communities for years to come. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/64498157/410286056/1504078835?refcode=EM_FR_230607NetPensFwd_N_HF_Actives_3_G_N_G1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDA1NzQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiN2MzZjQ1NDgtOWMwNS1lZTExLTkwN2MtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=6tqAJBBtvZt4z13GNRBztp2-4DXo8jUdXQPCCK31s34=) This marks a critical milestone, and I’m proud to have been a part of this work to end invasive net pen fish farming in Washington’s waterways.

****[Will you read my team’s message below to see why this work is so important and then, if you’re able, donate $10 so I can keep pushing for epic progress just like this?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/64498158/410286059/1504078835?refcode=EM_FR_230607NetPensFwd_N_HF_Actives_3_G_N_L1&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDA1NzQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiN2MzZjQ1NDgtOWMwNS1lZTExLTkwN2MtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=6tqAJBBtvZt4z13GNRBztp2-4DXo8jUdXQPCCK31s34=)**** **[DONATE $10](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/64498161/410286062/1504078835?refcode=EM_FR_230607NetPensFwd_N_HF_Actives_3_G_N_B1&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDA1NzQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiN2MzZjQ1NDgtOWMwNS1lZTExLTkwN2MtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=6tqAJBBtvZt4z13GNRBztp2-4DXo8jUdXQPCCK31s34=)**

Hilary _On May 5, Team Franz wrote:_ | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/64498163/410286065/1504078835?refcode=EM_FR_230607NetPensFwd_N_HF_Actives_3_G_N_Logo2&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDA1NzQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiN2MzZjQ1NDgtOWMwNS1lZTExLTkwN2MtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=6tqAJBBtvZt4z13GNRBztp2-4DXo8jUdXQPCCK31s34=)

Peter,

Following a years-long battle to protect and defend our local waters, work is underway to remove the final invasive net pens from Puget Sound. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/64498165/410286067/1504078835?refcode=EM_FR_230607NetPensFwd_N_HF_Actives_3_G_N_G2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDA1NzQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiN2MzZjQ1NDgtOWMwNS1lZTExLTkwN2MtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=6tqAJBBtvZt4z13GNRBztp2-4DXo8jUdXQPCCK31s34=) **Hilary has been at the forefront of this fight to end net pen fishing, and this marks an enormous victory in our efforts to support our waters, fishers, Tribes, and native salmon. **** **

The removal of these net pens is another example of Hilary’s commitment to protecting our environment and standing up to corporations who threaten them, and she is determined to continue this critical work as governor. ****[Will you pitch in what you can so Hilary can continue fighting for the sustainability of our waters, lands, and communities?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/64498167/410286070/1504078835?refcode=EM_FR_230607NetPensFwd_N_HF_Actives_3_G_N_L2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDA1NzQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiN2MzZjQ1NDgtOWMwNS1lZTExLTkwN2MtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=6tqAJBBtvZt4z13GNRBztp2-4DXo8jUdXQPCCK31s34=)**** The Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) has been accused of funneling money to organizations that were helping immigrants **illegally cross our southern border.** _**YES, you read that correctly... the Biden administration is using _YOUR TAX DOLLARS_ to fund illegal immigration.**_ [_**I need 1,000 patriots to sign my petition and condemn Biden's mismanagement of our taxes and our border.**_](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=f9ef163c38f94024905cc6b5e55f222e&_m=88b683f20bdc45a880fa590d2d2d15f5&_e=AWs8B1Ykokj-Rlh3AsMrmenEHzGuK-7IqEU9_O2hZuQEyAIMbhPIF0yQY-6g9JRwmPQPzeD2fHm_jlJwkNWoEoKqqD8k24FrR8GrjfldtPifTTKoSzCQjI_jQh-KnIxM0Sh3_iGJ_A6H6inRokahP1sdnQRc6mNT9Y67aryZm5er-gWsslyxn_vZoUHRPH4EwcJWv6uMXhUeHvQCunKTn0fRcI_LFETXO2AhDgzG9MrFi5HqRcIo8vvnS7NuXLes)

subject: Cornel West can force a reckoning
body:
Listen now (14 min) | The Democratic Party's theatrical charade co-opting minority communities cannot survive the challenge that Dr. West is poised to mount.

[Cornel West can force a reckoning](https://substack..nnHr7LHK7uV_HZllFTS36op1BNd3Z6Km9jU2M5MN1wI?)
The Democratic Party's theatrical charade co-opting minority communities cannot survive the challenge that Dr. West is poised to mount.
| | [Listen to preview](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=126552992&utm_source=podcast-email&play_audio=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTI2NTUyOTkyLCJpYXQiOjE2ODYxOTkyNzcsImV4cCI6MTY4ODc5MTI3NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.v9l2kzrom-S9QIKRgCyatUfQUqUsPjLDOZbXtCRHnjE&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email#play)|

[](https://substack.com/profile/15484941-shahid-buttar)| | Shahid Buttar| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=126552992&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTI2NTUyOTkyLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTY4NjE5OTI3NywiZXhwIjoxNjg4NzkxMjc3LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTAxODU5NyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.5KcsEaS2_ys5zg55d2QWbygfS0D91rSwnMYBYiRhIsw&utm_medium=email&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=126552992&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTI2NTUyOTkyLCJpYXQiOjE2ODYxOTkyNzcsImV4cCI6MTY4ODc5MTI3NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.v9l2kzrom-S9QIKRgCyatUfQUqUsPjLDOZbXtCRHnjE&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=126552992&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTI2NTUyOTkyLCJpYXQiOjE2ODYxOTkyNzcsImV4cCI6MTY4ODc5MTI3NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.v9l2kzrom-S9QIKRgCyatUfQUqUsPjLDOZbXtCRHnjE)
The 2024 presidential election is shaping up like a remix of _Titanic_ and the _[Keystone Cops](https://substack.cc/redirect/17316a1a-209a-4cb3-b754-fe96b877cadd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_ (or, for another generation, maybe [Reno 911](https://substack.cc/redirect/ade3c7c9-884f-46f4-9c29-3558648fd848?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)). But while an election-without-debates is increasingly recognized as farcically theatrical, a welcome announcement from America's leading moral philosopher could turn it into a real race, something more like _Star Wars Episode I: The Phantom Menace_ (or perhaps instead _[Cannonball Run](https://substack.cc/redirect/d59defed-b3c9-436f-8c87-55a58cb31c8a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_.
[T]he most immediate impact of Dr. West's entry into the 2024 presidential campaign may be its exposure of the sleight of hand long enabled by the Congressional Black Caucus.
### A ruse on which Democrats rely
Joe Biden has long claimed to be a supporter of communities of color, despite a long record of enabling predatory policies from the notorious [1993 crime bill](https://substack.cc/redirect/979ca024-30b5-4cb4-822e-e0a467537d17?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to the [dismantling of welfare](https://substack.cc/redirect/10955d62-cb83-465b-a610-7ccbd8cb72aa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) under the Clinton administration. His record as a president has unfortunately continued the pattern, from his continuation (until just a few weeks ago) of Trump [border policies](https://substack.cc/redirect/0c5b652b-e27c-4d98-b0d6-61b31a6d2bba?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to his abandonment of working Americans on issues from [student debt](https://substack.cc/redirect/1c75a87d-d579-49ca-971e-26c12a9b5504?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) relief to preventable [hunger among the elderly](https://substack.cc/redirect/81735078-85a8-40ae-a708-7474f6097e9a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Elected Black leaders with an equally long history of placing their careers before their constituents have been quick to overlook Biden's record, currying favor with the establishment they have critically enabled for decades--at [enormous cost](https://substack.cc/redirect/908b2e96-79e7-4a4c-86bc-980023804aa5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to democracy and the future.
The farce that they together maintain in order to secure the crucial support of Black voters is only possible in the context of an electoral vacuum. The party establishment aims to maintain that vacuum by, for instance, denying voters debates among the contestants in the presidential primary, treating Biden's nomination as a foregone conclusion, and deploying any number of spokespeople to smear his grassroots opponents.
I'll leave aside for now a detailed analysis of how profoundly idiotic that strategy is. But given the weakness in Biden's public support, and his abject failure to live up to even his meager campaign promises, nominating Biden again would be tantamount to handing the GOP the keys to the White House.
His international laughingstock of a Vice President is not poised to help. Kamala Harris is a walking reflection of a strategically confused Democratic Party attempt to replicate Barack Obama. But the shoe does not fit. Harris' attempts to appear inspiring instead reveal her reliance on speechwriters and nervous laughter. She and Pete Buttigieg could start a master class in how to say nothing at all while filling air time. Together, they embody everything wrong with the Democratic Party and its vacuous commitment to style and identity over substantive interests.
The point, for now, is simply that the "Democratic" Party's relationship to democracy is similar to the relationship between "correctional" institutions and the notion of correcting anything. It is a party branded around a principle that it routinely disrespects--and has successfully degraded.
I would use the image of Democrats spitting in the faces of voters, but the metaphor of shooting themselves (and unfortunately everyone else) in the feet is sadly more apt.
Know someone who might be informed by this post? It's public, so feel free to share it.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=126552992&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTI2NTUyOTkyLCJpYXQiOjE2ODYxOTkyNzcsImV4cCI6MTY4ODc5MTI3NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.v9l2kzrom-S9QIKRgCyatUfQUqUsPjLDOZbXtCRHnjE)
### A prophetic voice impossible to ignore
In the face of this demagoguery enabled by the "Democratic" Party, democracy gained a step this week in the [entrance of a new voice in the presidential race](https://substack.cc/redirect/1c69e23e-24c7-4ee4-815f-fa934f24b34f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Unlike his predecessors, he is poised to break the vacuum and force the debate that the Democratic establishment has done everything conceivably possible to avoid--from [fabricating](https://substack.cc/redirect/fea275d9-5eae-41e5-a872-2cbf7caf7e9c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) orchestrated disinformation to [coronating](https://substack.cc/redirect/420d7bd6-55ca-4252-9bf1-d1a74c773917?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) candidates despite layers of [documented](https://substack.cc/redirect/db29a4cc-c128-4ae2-a58d-7d2efb7b9eda?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [potential further](https://substack.cc/redirect/c40a91a7-94cc-4544-9880-141de962b366?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) corruption.
Dr. Cornel West is a peerless visionary. His ideas could transform the United States even more dramatically than the vision that Bernie Sanders articulated. He can eloquently address a limitless range of issues, and perceives politics through the crystal clear lens of a moral philosophy grounded in human rights and dignity. Were he to win the White House, he could do more than anyone has in decades to hold America to our own stated principles.
[https://twitter.com/CornelWest/status/1665743761551548416?s=20](https://substack.cc/redirect/b9d455f6-66e0-470b-a0ae-3458f1a71c49?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [Note: Substack and Twitter are locked in something akin to a digital arms race, which is why you can't see here the video featuring Dr. West announcing his campaign. You can, however, still access it through the publicly accessible link preceding this note. I encourage every reader to lend him your ear.]
Dissident writer Chris Hedges, who served as the former Middle East Bureau Chief and Balkan Bureau Chief for the _New York Times_ , [wrote at some length](https://substack.cc/redirect/242e5ce4-ee6b-4720-b94a-fb7774aedb22?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) about Dr. West's powerful insight and compelling concerns. This particular passage stood out to me:
> "Progressives in the Democratic Party think they can get away with rendering invisible their consensus to extravagant militarism," he said. "But it goes hand-in-hand with the U.S. imperial policy. It goes hand-in-hand with [expansion](https://substack.cc/redirect/d22c54f7-3590-451d-ba3e-337975372d5e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of NATO. It goes hand-in-hand to lead us in the proxy war with Russia. It goes hand-in-hand with taking money out of programs that have to do with education and healthcare and jobs with a living wage and housing, basic social needs of, not just people in this country, in the empire, but around the world."
> "How many precious Iraqis were killed by the U.S. war machine?" he asked. "Each life is precious. Iraqi lives have the same value as a life anywhere else in the world. How many lives were killed in Afghanistan? And Libya? We can look at all of the different examples. Then you've got examples in Haiti, you've got examples in Panama, you've got examples in Grenada and so forth in the last forty or fifty years. And we're not even talking about the co-ordinated activities of overthrowing democratic regimes in Iran and in other places. These are the kinds of issues we're going to have to hit head on my brother, the same is true in terms of the Middle East. The monies we give to Egypt, the monies we give to Israel. How can we render invisible the suffering of our precious Palestinian brothers and sisters given the U.S. complicity and endorsement of these vicious apartheid-like conditions? There's no way that the Democratic Party can get away with this anymore."
> "We had the same problem of the Democratic Party deferring to the apartheid regime in South Africa," he went on. "What did we have to do? We came out with boycotts and sanctions. We came out with divestments. Well, the same is true now in apartheid-like conditions in the West Bank and Gaza. We can do that without in any way falling prey to one of the more vicious ideologies of the last two thousand years, which is the hatred of Jews. We don't have a minute to engage in any kind of anti-Jewish hatred or anti-Jewish sentiment, but at the same time we don't have a minute to turn our backs to the suffering of Palestinians tied to U.S. foreign policy that always looks away from their suffering, looks away from their social misery, looks away from the murders taking place, looks away from the houses that are crushed, looks away from the land that is taken, and so forth. Those are the kinds of issues that we have to bring to the public with whatever integrity, honesty and decency that we have and that's pretty much what the tradition that produced me is all about, from Frederick Douglass to [Ella Baker](https://substack.cc/redirect/40dedb22-6d1d-4d10-b6e3-a0853931cf55?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)."
No voice in American politics can rival either Dr. West's poetry, or his prose.
There are other candidates in the presidential race--even the Democratic primary whose legitimacy Dr. West is poised to help challenge--who I support as better alternatives to Biden. One of them is Marianne Williamson. While her foreign policy vision has deferred at times to the Washington establishment, she shares Dr. West's commitments to economic justice and environmental sustainability. In dramatic contrast, elected Democrats mouth those words with self-serving frequency, while relying on the public's ignorance to get away with routinely betraying their stated commitments.
Notwithstanding Dr. West's unparalleled vision, and the hope his campaign could inspire in a generation thirsty for alternatives to ecocide-for-profit, the most immediate impact of Dr. West's entry into the 2024 presidential race may be its exposure of the sleight of hand long enabled by the Congressional Black Caucus.
My work aims to expose layers of nuance that media sources have proven themselves institutionally incapable of thoughtfully addressing. Sign up for a free (or paid, if you're able) subscription to receive new posts and gain insight beyond the headlines!
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..VlnOA1vkb-3S7u0wRbQNLVegicc32EjZxdex9PU8pK4?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=126552992)
Candidates running outside the party apparatus confront a tilted landscape, including not only a weaponized press establishment, campaign finance rules that effectively invite corporations and billionaires to buy elections, and a formal process that will include no televised debates, but also the limited imagination of a body politic that is equal parts complacent, ignorant, and complicit. That spectrum of challenges has created opportunities for careerists in Congress to advance themselves while abandoning their own communities and supporters.
For instance, in 2020, voices from the Congressional Black Caucus--especially Jim Clyburn (D-SC)--deployed their substantial influence to convince their supporters to vote against their own interests. They forcefully pushed the candidate whose path to and through Washington relied on support from Wall Street, who reciprocated with countless acts of servitude to capital, put Clarence Thomas on the Supreme Court, played a crucial role in creating a slave labor force by imprisoning millions of Americans, and supports predatory for-profit "medicine."
A crushing irony emerges when considering how desperately Clyburn's supporters need support from government, the kind of support that only change in Washington can enable.
West entering the race represents a stick confronting the Democratic Party establishment, in the form of a credible threat to peel off crucial Black voters on whom it relies. Not only do Biden and the Democratic establishment writ large rely on Black voters, but everyone knows it--including Black voters.
The Democratic Party has [declared in federal court that its establishment can pick any candidate they want, whatever the voters think](https://substack.cc/redirect/8c9a35c0-0283-4a09-b691-75f94616f61c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and has won the right to effectively make up its rules as it goes to favor candidates supported by Wall Street. Be that as it may, Democrats committed to coronating Biden despite his record and lack of public support are hurtling towards a cliff.
West's analysis could not only expose the predatory conservatism of Biden's record over decades, but also force Biden to respond to the concerns of communities of color who he has engaged only theatrically. As a candidate running outside the corporate duopoly, West has already [committed to staying in the election](https://substack.cc/redirect/6bff4329-11e1-421f-871a-45fbe9dc0853?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) until November 2024, drawing a sharp contrast with Bernie Sanders' unfortunately premature concession in spring 2020.
Biden's enablers would do well to remember their reliance on voters who West is particularly poised to reach.
### West already won the debate--even if Biden tries to duck it
In an earlier era, the word "woke" was coined to refer, more or less, to people who understood the vision that Dr. Cornel West has long promoted. The co-optation of that term to instead connote the identitarian theater to which the Democratic Party has long reduced itself reflects the collapse of whatever claims to be the Left in the United States.
With enough support from enough concerned people, Dr. West could sufficiently threaten the Democratic Party's interests--specifically by exposing Biden's hypocrisy to Black voters--to force a debate at the national level.
However the term "woke" may have been co-opted, weaponized and contorted, Dr. West's vision remains not only compelling, but also crucial for the ultimate liberation of Americans bearing the burden of either dark skin or inopportune circumstances.
His voice will be a force impossible to ignore.
That's a good thing for democracy in America, however many Democrats ironically (prove through their actions that they) despise it.
Please join me in supporting Dr. West's important campaign! He will need the support of every American concerned about the future to overcome the bipartisan weight of Wall Street. Visit his campaign website at [https://www.cornelwest24.com](https://substack.cc/redirect/2eee0adb-67e3-4fb7-8a92-4fd2cc92f48f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to learn more and get involved.
| [](https://substack.cc/redirect/1640b471-5db1-4d54-80a0-d4eee16b4c23?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
www.cornelwest24.com
With enough support from e concerned people, Dr. West could sufficiently threaten the Democratic Party's interests--specifically by exposing Biden's hypocrisy to Black voters--to force a debate at the national level.
Paid subscribers can watch a video of Dr. West embracing me and speaking in support of my congressional campaign in 2020, and also read the story of how we first met on a dance floor and bonded over our shared appreciation for Prince. Dr. West's later endorsement was among the ones of which I was ultimately most proud...

clean_body: Listen now (14 min) | The Democratic Party's theatrical charade co-opting minority communities cannot survive the challenge that Dr. West is poised to mount. | | ---|---|--- | | | or ? ? | | Jun 8 ? The Democratic Party's theatrical charade co-opting minority communities cannot survive the challenge that Dr. West is poised to mount. | | | ---|---|--- | | Shahid Buttar| | | | | | The 2024 presidential election is shaping up like a remix of Titanic and the (or, for another generation, maybe ). But while an election-without-debates is increasingly recognized as farcically theatrical, a welcome announcement from America's leading moral philosopher could turn it into a real race, something more like Star Wars Episode I: The Phantom Menace (or perhaps instead ). [T]he most immediate impact of Dr. West's entry into the 2024 presidential campaign may be its exposure of the sleight of hand long enabled by the Congressional Black Caucus. A ruse on which Democrats rely Joe Biden has long claimed to be a supporter of communities of color, despite a long record of enabling predatory policies from the notorious to the under the Clinton administration. His record as a president has unfortunately continued the pattern, from his continuation (until just a few weeks ago) of Trump to his abandonment of working Americans on issues from relief to preventable . Elected Black leaders with an equally long history of placing their careers before their constituents have been quick to overlook Biden's record, currying favor with the establishment they have critically enabled for decades--at to democracy and the future. The farce that they together maintain in order to secure the crucial support of Black voters is only possible in the context of an electoral vacuum. The party establishment aims to maintain that vacuum by, for instance, denying voters debates among the contestants in the presidential primary, treating Biden's nomination as a foregone conclusion, and deploying any number of spokespeople to smear his grassroots opponents. I'll leave aside for now a detailed analysis of how profoundly idiotic that strategy is. But given the weakness in Biden's public support, and his abject failure to live up to even his meager campaign promises, nominating Biden again would be tantamount to handing the GOP the keys to the White House. His international laughingstock of a Vice President is not poised to help. Kamala Harris is a walking reflection of a strategically confused Democratic Party attempt to replicate Barack Obama. But the shoe does not fit. Harris' attempts to appear inspiring instead reveal her reliance on speechwriters and nervous laughter. She and Pete Buttigieg could start a master class in how to say nothing at all while filling air time. Together, they embody everything wrong with the Democratic Party and its vacuous commitment to style and identity over substantive interests. The point, for now, is simply that the "Democratic" Party's relationship to democracy is similar to the relationship between "correctional" institutions and the notion of correcting anything. It is a party branded around a principle that it routinely disrespects--and has successfully degraded. I would use the image of Democrats spitting in the faces of voters, but the metaphor of shooting themselves (and unfortunately everyone else) in the feet is sadly more apt. Know someone who might be informed by this post? It's public, so feel free to share it. A prophetic voice impossible to ignore In the face of this demagoguery enabled by the "Democratic" Party, democracy gained a step this week in the . Unlike his predecessors, he is poised to break the vacuum and force the debate that the Democratic establishment has done everything conceivably possible to avoid--from orchestrated disinformation to candidates despite layers of and corruption. Dr. Cornel West is a peerless visionary. His ideas could transform the United States even more dramatically than the vision that Bernie Sanders articulated. He can eloquently address a limitless range of issues, and perceives politics through the crystal clear lens of a moral philosophy grounded in human rights and dignity. Were he to win the White House, he could do more than anyone has in decades to hold America to our own stated principles. [Note: Substack and Twitter are locked in something akin to a digital arms race, which is why you can't see here the video featuring Dr. West announcing his campaign. You can, however, still access it through the publicly accessible link preceding this note. I encourage every reader to lend him your ear.] Dissident writer Chris Hedges, who served as the former Middle East Bureau Chief and Balkan Bureau Chief for the New York Times , about Dr. West's powerful insight and compelling concerns. This particular passage stood out to me: "Progressives in the Democratic Party think they can get away with rendering invisible their consensus to extravagant militarism," he said. "But it goes hand-in-hand with the U.S. imperial policy. It goes hand-in-hand with of NATO. It goes hand-in-hand to lead us in the proxy war with Russia. It goes hand-in-hand with taking money out of programs that have to do with education and healthcare and jobs with a living wage and housing, basic social needs of, not just people in this country, in the empire, but around the world." "How many precious Iraqis were killed by the U.S. war machine?" he asked. "Each life is precious. Iraqi lives have the same value as a life anywhere else in the world. How many lives were killed in Afghanistan? And Libya? We can look at all of the different examples. Then you've got examples in Haiti, you've got examples in Panama, you've got examples in Grenada and so forth in the last forty or fifty years. And we're not even talking about the co-ordinated activities of overthrowing democratic regimes in Iran and in other places. These are the kinds of issues we're going to have to hit head on my brother, the same is true in terms of the Middle East. The monies we give to Egypt, the monies we give to Israel. How can we render invisible the suffering of our precious Palestinian brothers and sisters given the U.S. complicity and endorsement of these vicious apartheid-like conditions? There's no way that the Democratic Party can get away with this anymore." "We had the same problem of the Democratic Party deferring to the apartheid regime in South Africa," he went on. "What did we have to do? We came out with boycotts and sanctions. We came out with divestments. Well, the same is true now in apartheid-like conditions in the West Bank and Gaza. We can do that without in any way falling prey to one of the more vicious ideologies of the last two thousand years, which is the hatred of Jews. We don't have a minute to engage in any kind of anti-Jewish hatred or anti-Jewish sentiment, but at the same time we don't have a minute to turn our backs to the suffering of Palestinians tied to U.S. foreign policy that always looks away from their suffering, looks away from their social misery, looks away from the murders taking place, looks away from the houses that are crushed, looks away from the land that is taken, and so forth. Those are the kinds of issues that we have to bring to the public with whatever integrity, honesty and decency that we have and that's pretty much what the tradition that produced me is all about, from Frederick Douglass to ." No voice in American politics can rival either Dr. West's poetry, or his prose. There are other candidates in the presidential race--even the Democratic primary whose legitimacy Dr. West is poised to help challenge--who I support as better alternatives to Biden. One of them is Marianne Williamson. While her foreign policy vision has deferred at times to the Washington establishment, she shares Dr. West's commitments to economic justice and environmental sustainability. In dramatic contrast, elected Democrats mouth those words with self-serving frequency, while relying on the public's ignorance to get away with routinely betraying their stated commitments. Notwithstanding Dr. West's unparalleled vision, and the hope his campaign could inspire in a generation thirsty for alternatives to ecocide-for-profit, the most immediate impact of Dr. West's entry into the 2024 presidential race may be its exposure of the sleight of hand long enabled by the Congressional Black Caucus. My work aims to expose layers of nuance that media sources have proven themselves institutionally incapable of thoughtfully addressing. Sign up for a free (or paid, if you're able) subscription to receive new posts and gain insight beyond the headlines! Candidates running outside the party apparatus confront a tilted landscape, including not only a weaponized press establishment, campaign finance rules that effectively invite corporations and billionaires to buy elections, and a formal process that will include no televised debates, but also the limited imagination of a body politic that is equal parts complacent, ignorant, and complicit. That spectrum of challenges has created opportunities for careerists in Congress to advance themselves while abandoning their own communities and supporters. For instance, in 2020, voices from the Congressional Black Caucus--especially Jim Clyburn (D-SC)--deployed their substantial influence to convince their supporters to vote against their own interests. They forcefully pushed the candidate whose path to and through Washington relied on support from Wall Street, who reciprocated with countless acts of servitude to capital, put Clarence Thomas on the Supreme Court, played a crucial role in creating a slave labor force by imprisoning millions of Americans, and supports predatory for-profit "medicine." A crushing irony emerges when considering how desperately Clyburn's supporters need support from government, the kind of support that only change in Washington can enable. West entering the race represents a stick confronting the Democratic Party establishment, in the form of a credible threat to peel off crucial Black voters on whom it relies. Not only do Biden and the Democratic establishment writ large rely on Black voters, but everyone knows it--including Black voters. The Democratic Party has , and has won the right to effectively make up its rules as it goes to favor candidates supported by Wall Street. Be that as it may, Democrats committed to coronating Biden despite his record and lack of public support are hurtling towards a cliff. West's analysis could not only expose the predatory conservatism of Biden's record over decades, but also force Biden to respond to the concerns of communities of color who he has engaged only theatrically. As a candidate running outside the corporate duopoly, West has already until November 2024, drawing a sharp contrast with Bernie Sanders' unfortunately premature concession in spring 2020. Biden's enablers would do well to remember their reliance on voters who West is particularly poised to reach. West already won the debate--even if Biden tries to duck it In an earlier era, the word "woke" was coined to refer, more or less, to people who understood the vision that Dr. Cornel West has long promoted. The co-optation of that term to instead connote the identitarian theater to which the Democratic Party has long reduced itself reflects the collapse of whatever claims to be the Left in the United States. With enough support from enough concerned people, Dr. West could sufficiently threaten the Democratic Party's interests--specifically by exposing Biden's hypocrisy to Black voters--to force a debate at the national level. However the term "woke" may have been co-opted, weaponized and contorted, Dr. West's vision remains not only compelling, but also crucial for the ultimate liberation of Americans bearing the burden of either dark skin or inopportune circumstances. His voice will be a force impossible to ignore. That's a good thing for democracy in America, however many Democrats ironically (prove through their actions that they) despise it. Please join me in supporting Dr. West's important campaign! He will need the support of every American concerned about the future to overcome the bipartisan weight of Wall Street. Visit his campaign website at to learn more and get involved. | | ---|---|--- www.cornelwest24.com With enough support from e concerned people, Dr. West could sufficiently threaten the Democratic Party's interests--specifically by exposing Biden's hypocrisy to Black voters--to force a debate at the national level. Paid subscribers can watch a video of Dr. West embracing me and speaking in support of my congressional campaign in 2020, and also read the story of how we first met on a dance floor and bonded over our shared appreciation for Prince. Dr. West's later endorsement was among the ones of which I was ultimately most proud... This will be a _**once-in-a-lifetime event**_ that could **challenge the very existence of America.**
Hi, I'm Lance Wallnau, Christian author and pastor for over thirty years. I'm one of only three evangelical leaders to accurately predict Donald Trump's Presidency.
And as scary as it is, I do believe this incoming catastrophe is unstoppable... **because it is a part of God's plan!**
You see, **we are under God's judgment**. Our government leaders and church leadership have not pushed back against the corruption, moral depravity, and outright evil behavior we see in culture. It seems like every day we are slipping closer and closer to Sodom and Gomorrah levels. God has responded by putting America under divine judgment! And this judgment seems to be starting with...
---

**A Rebalancing Of The Financial Scales**

**A Rebalancing Of The Financial Scales**
| | | Which means **everything falsely overvalued will plummet**. And there is nothing more overvalued than _**the U.S. Dollar!**_
After all, other countries only use our dollar because they have to. _(To trade with each other.)_
But God is judging America and our leadership position is fading away. I think very soon that means **the value of the dollar will be crashing, and the prices for everything will sky-rocket.**
Imagine waking up one morning and all the essentials (rent, food, gas, medications) are almost _**impossible to afford!**_
It breaks my heart knowing people are about to be forced into desperate situations. But this is exactly what happens when God's judgment is in full swing.
_**So is there any way to prepare? YES...**_
---

**What Christians Have Done
For Thousand Of Years...**

I am convinced this is a God-sent opportunity for Christians to safeguard their livelihood. _So don't let it slip away!_
**[Click here to request your free 20-page info kit on gold IRAs.](http://trk.faithandlibertymail.-mLNhJg)**

Lance Wallnau

name: Yesli Vega
subject: I need your help
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF YESLI VEGA
body:
| | |
---
|
---
| Dear Friends,
Our beloved Prince William County is at an inflection point. Five months from yesterday will be the most important local election in recent memory.
Will your tax bills continue to go up by an average of $2,200 every four years while data centers still pay half of what they do in Loudoun and Fairfax?
Will our 70% increase in violent crime over the last four years continue to skyrocket and compound?
Will data centers and citizen-last land use continue to pave over every last square inch of green space in the County?
Prince William County is a beautiful place. Stretching from Bull Run Mountain and the Rural Crescent in the west to the Potomac River in the east.
Folks like you chose to move here due to the relatively low cost of living and tax climate, low crime, and sensible land-use policies and development.
That has all changed in the last four years and will only get worse unless we change course.
That's why I'm asking for your help today. The Coles District is the quintessential "swing" district, vital to ensuring a majority that puts our citizens first when it comes to issues of taxes, crime, land-use, fiscal responsibility, and overall good government.
[Click here to join in this fight to take back our County today](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001hXhgw_oKOPqWKMgjBC1Ql_SwJlSk4gFAXTzybpD3wvrcyNOswdKLZ-oDIecYKvccm7nKi5qWsZ7hsFXRkjWvxKoX1x3qzbO_hk-JujrBHuwe-S-nIYpeeoOaQZkyL4qsg8oJic8Znv6DsPFc_fc2EgDbq_qgtAHVskHwP0ulsuTx8rieNt0DJJcGmyP4d03k&c=bNDxoUpI7N_de8V5RKjIJKZQ5mfz_qNC1yjxcKRLhGtPsNXGH8NCMw==&ch=MLM-EKe2VAK_cssZ3QYkqE0i-nhRciKkrGpHv2TsYMzOGkfyA2An7Q==)[!](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001hXhgw_oKOPqWKMgjBC1Ql_SwJlSk4gFAXTzybpD3wvrcyNOswdKLZ-oDIecYKvccm7nKi5qWsZ7hsFXRkjWvxKoX1x3qzbO_hk-JujrBHuwe-S-nIYpeeoOaQZkyL4qsg8oJic8Znv6DsPFc_fc2EgDbq_qgtAHVskHwP0ulsuTx8rieNt0DJJcGmyP4d03k&c=bNDxoUpI7N_de8V5RKjIJKZQ5mfz_qNC1yjxcKRLhGtPsNXGH8NCMw==&ch=MLM-EKe2VAK_cssZ3QYkqE0i-nhRciKkrGpHv2TsYMzOGkfyA2An7Q==)
Four years ago, it cost $150,000 to win this seat for the principles we hold dear. Principles that aren't partisan, but about the quality of life we all want for ourselves, children, and grandchildren.
We have a state-imposed fundraising deadline today and I would be honored to earn your financial support at any level possible.
[You may click here to make a contribution today](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001hXhgw_oKOPqWKMgjBC1Ql_SwJlSk4gFAXTzybpD3wvrcyNOswdKLZ-oDIecYKvccm7nKi5qWsZ7hsFXRkjWvxKoX1x3qzbO_hk-JujrBHuwe-S-nIYpeeoOaQZkyL4qsg8oJic8Znv6DsPFc_fc2EgDbq_qgtAHVskHwP0ulsuTx8rieNt0DJJcGmyP4d03k&c=bNDxoUpI7N_de8V5RKjIJKZQ5mfz_qNC1yjxcKRLhGtPsNXGH8NCMw==&ch=MLM-EKe2VAK_cssZ3QYkqE0i-nhRciKkrGpHv2TsYMzOGkfyA2An7Q==).
Every dollar, big and small, will go a long way towards helping us compete against the special interest groups that are planning on spending seven figures in Prince William County this year to maintain the status quo.
A status quo reality that is...

* A 70% increase in violent crime and the deadliest five-month period in Prince William County's history (Jan-May 2023)
* A $2,200 increase in the average tax bill
* Unchecked development by billion-dollar companies receiving tax breaks on the backs of our residents

Enough is enough.
It's time to take Prince William County back for the citizens we represent.
[Please help me to continue that fight today with your most generous contribution possible](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001hXhgw_oKOPqWKMgjBC1Ql_SwJlSk4gFAXTzybpD3wvrcyNOswdKLZ-oDIecYKvccm7nKi5qWsZ7hsFXRkjWvxKoX1x3qzbO_hk-JujrBHuwe-S-nIYpeeoOaQZkyL4qsg8oJic8Znv6DsPFc_fc2EgDbq_qgtAHVskHwP0ulsuTx8rieNt0DJJcGmyP4d03k&c=bNDxoUpI7N_de8V5RKjIJKZQ5mfz_qNC1yjxcKRLhGtPsNXGH8NCMw==&ch=MLM-EKe2VAK_cssZ3QYkqE0i-nhRciKkrGpHv2TsYMzOGkfyA2An7Q==).
Thank you.
For Coles,
Yesli Vega Prince William County Supervisor Coles District

clean_body: | | | | | Dear Friends, Our beloved Prince William County is at an inflection point. Five months from yesterday will be the most important local election in recent memory. Will your tax bills continue to go up by an average of $2,200 every four years while data centers still pay half of what they do in Loudoun and Fairfax? Will our 70% increase in violent crime over the last four years continue to skyrocket and compound? Will data centers and citizen-last land use continue to pave over every last square inch of green space in the County? Prince William County is a beautiful place. Stretching from Bull Run Mountain and the Rural Crescent in the west to the Potomac River in the east. Folks like you chose to move here due to the relatively low cost of living and tax climate, low crime, and sensible land-use policies and development. That has all changed in the last four years and will only get worse unless we change course. That's why I'm asking for your help today. The Coles District is the quintessential "swing" district, vital to ensuring a majority that puts our citizens first when it comes to issues of taxes, crime, land-use, fiscal responsibility, and overall good government. == Four years ago, it cost $150,000 to win this seat for the principles we hold dear. Principles that aren't partisan, but about the quality of life we all want for ourselves, children, and grandchildren. We have a state-imposed fundraising deadline today and I would be honored to earn your financial support at any level possible. ==. Every dollar, big and small, will go a long way towards helping us compete against the special interest groups that are planning on spending seven figures in Prince William County this year to maintain the status quo. A status quo reality that is... A 70% increase in violent crime and the deadliest five-month period in Prince William County's history (Jan-May 2023) A $2,200 increase in the average tax bill * Unchecked development by billion-dollar companies receiving tax breaks on the backs of our residents Enough is enough. It's time to take Prince William County back for the citizens we represent. ==. Thank you.
